summary: Matched several fighter color-animation routines in ftcolanim.c, including ft_800BFF34, ftCo_800C0200, ftCo_800C0358, and ftCo_800C0408. The PR also tightened callback typing for lb_80014258 by introducing FtCmd2, and cleaned up an item color-overlay loop in it_2725.c to use GET_ITEM and the now-correct callback signature.
what_changed: Added a new FtCmd2 callback typedef in ft/forward.h with signature void (*)(Fighter_GObj*, CommandInfo*, int), while simplifying FtCmd parameter names.
Changed lb_80014258 in lb_00F9.h from accepting FtCmd to accepting FtCmd2, matching call sites that pass callbacks receiving a third integer argument.
Implemented static ft_800BFF34 as a dispatcher that subtracts 0x15 from the callback argument and indexes ftCo_803C6AD0.
Made ft_800BFF70 static and kept it as a similar dispatcher into ftCo_803C6ADC using arg0 - 21.

Matched ftCo_800C0200, including bounds checking for arg1 >= 0x7B, OSReport/assert diagnostics for spycloak color animation, color-overlay reset via lb_80014498, and reapplication of related fighter color-animation states.
Matched ftCo_800C0358, copying either x488 or x408 color-overlay state between fighters depending on Fighter_804D653C[arg2].unk5.

Matched ftCo_800C0408, iterating x508, x408, and x488 color overlays with lb_80014258, dispatching color-animation callbacks, resetting overlays, restoring special color states, and setting fp->x2221_b3 true.
Added includes for ft/ftdata.h and ftCo_HammerWait.h needed by the matched ftcolanim code.
Removed explicit literal globals for the OSReport/assert strings and used string literals directly in the matched code.
Updated it_80279BE0 in it_2725.c to initialize Item* with GET_ITEM(item_gobj), pass item_gobj directly to lb_80014258, and remove an explicit function-pointer cast.
smart_moves: Introduced FtCmd2 rather than forcing mismatched casts at call sites, making lb_80014258's real callback ABI explicit.

Used small static dispatcher helpers for callback table selection, matching the observed pattern where lb_80014258 supplies an integer command value that is normalized before indexing ftCo_803C6AD0 or ftCo_803C6ADC.
Preserved assert/OSReport behavior and exact diagnostic strings for invalid spycloak color-animation reset cases.
Applied #pragma dont_inline around the newly matched ftcolanim block, indicating inlining control was needed for the functions to match.
Cleaned up item color-overlay code after fixing the shared callback type, eliminating a manual cast and using GET_ITEM for established item access style.
decomp_lessons: When a shared library helper invokes callbacks with more arguments than an existing typedef represents, add the accurate typedef and update the helper prototype instead of hiding the mismatch with casts.
Color-overlay processing in fighter code uses repeated lb_80014258 loops followed by lb_80014498 resets; preserving this loop structure can be important for matching.
Fighter color-animation state restoration is conditional on several packed fighter flags and fields, including x2226_b4, x2224_b2, dmg.x18F0, x221D_b6, x1994, x1990, x2221_b0, and x408.x28_colanim.i.
Bounds checks with OSReport plus __assert may correspond to original source diagnostics; matching them directly can remove the need for separate forced literal symbols.
Callback dispatch tables with magic offsets such as arg - 0x15 or arg - 21 should be represented directly when they explain table indexing and improve matchability.
naming_conventions: New callback typedefs follow existing fighter typedef style: FtCmd and FtCmd2.
Unmatched or partially understood fighter globals and fields retain project placeholder names such as Fighter_804D653C, ftData_UnkMotionStates4, x408, x488, x508, and x2221_b3.
Item access uses the existing GET_ITEM(item_gobj) macro rather than manual user_data assignment when possible.
ftCo_ prefix is retained for common fighter routines, while address-based names remain for still-unidentified functions such as ftCo_800C0200 and ftCo_800C0408.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around the matched ftcolanim implementations to control compiler inlining.
Converted former decomp stubs marked with /// #function into C implementations while keeping local static linkage where appropriate.
Matched callback table dispatch by computing an index from the third callback argument before calling through FtCmd arrays.
Removed unnecessary casts in it_80279BE0 after correcting the lb_80014258 prototype, likely improving type-driven codegen and readability.
Used direct string literals in OSReport and __assert calls instead of forced literal variables, while still preserving the diagnostic contents.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human text excerpt is empty, so no substantive feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other lb_80014258 call sites that still use casts or callbacks matching FtCmd2.
Investigate whether FtCmd2 should be renamed once the third callback argument semantics are understood.
Identify the meaning of Fighter_804D653C[arg].unk5 in fighter color-animation selection.
Check whether ftCo_800C0200, ftCo_800C0358, and ftCo_800C0408 can receive descriptive names after more ftcolanim behavior is documented.
Search for other color-overlay loops that should use GET_ITEM or equivalent typed accessors instead of manual user_data casts.
